interpreted_controls.control_objective: To ensure that all applicable KYC requirements relating to 'Customer Due Diligence', thereby enabling bank to demonstrate full regulatory adherence and reduce Compliance Risk exposure.
interpreted_controls.control_scope: Applicable entities: Bank, Reserve Bank, Customer. Responsible departments: Compliance. Regulatory domain: KYC.
interpreted_controls.automation_rationale: Obligation fulfilment involves organisational processes and human decisions that cannot be fully automated.

interpreted_controls.control_objective: To govern permissible exceptions to all applicable KYC requirements relating to 'Know Your Customer (KYC) Compliance', thereby enabling bank to demonstrate full regulatory adherence and reduce Operational Risk exposure.
interpreted_controls.control_scope: Applicable entities: Bank, Customer. Responsible departments: Compliance, Risk, Operations. Regulatory domain: KYC, Digital Payments.
interpreted_controls.automation_rationale: This control type relies primarily on human judgment and process adherence, limiting automation applicability.
interpreted_controls.business_rationale: Inadequate customer due diligence exposes the institution to exploitation by financial criminals. 'Know Your Customer (KYC) Compliance' ensures that customer identities are verified and risk-profiled before account opening, protecting the institution from being used as a conduit for money laundering, terrorist financing, or fraud — all of which carry severe regulatory and reputational consequences.

Implementation follows the 'Workflow Creation' pattern and is owned by the Compliance function. Compliance is verified through manual audit.
